##NAMES=Lavinskyite
##RRUFFID=R141115
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=K(LiCu)Cu_6_(Si_4_O_11_)_2_(OH)_4_
##LOCALITY=Wessels mine, South Africa
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Jaco and Tania Janse van Nieuwenhuizen
##DESCRIPTION=Greenish blue or light blue platy crystals, associated with dark blue scottyite (R141116) and blue wesselsite (with cleavage)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R141115
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(K_0.98_Sr_0.01_)(Li_1.25_Cu_0.71_Na_0.08_)Cu_6_(Si_4.03_O_11_)_2_(OH)_4_ ; (Lavinskyite is the dark grey material in the image, associated with light grey R141116 scottyite)
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 19.048 b: 20.386 c: 5.2485 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 2038.1 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
